rows.name: Constellation Software Inc.
rows.last_memo: Constellation's stock jumped about 6.5% today with no apparent reason — no earnings, no acquisition news, no management update. For a company like this, where what matters is whether management keeps buying good software businesses at attractive prices and compounding free cash f

rows.name: Deckers Outdoor Corp.
rows.last_memo: Jefferies, a major investment bank, upgraded its rating on Deckers and pointed to HOKA's growth as the main reason. This is good news in the sense that smart external analysts agree with our thinking, but it doesn't actually tell us anything new about the business. The stock is a

rows.name: Meta Platforms Inc.
rows.last_memo: Meta is building one of the largest data centers ever constructed in Texas, and a BlackRock-managed fund is raising $12 billion in bonds to pay for it. Think of it like a landlord building a skyscraper specifically for one tenant — Meta gets the computing power without directly b
risks: Off-balance-sheet financing structures (BlackRock bond vehicle) may obscure Meta's true economic leverage; operating or financing lease obligations could materially exceed the reported 0.05x net debt/EBITDA if aggregated with other similar arrangements.
Concentration of AI compute in a single large Texas facility introduces geographic and operational concentration risk (power availability, weather, regulatory).
If AI revenue monetization lags the capex build-out, free cash flow could be pressured for multiple years before returns materialize, testing the patience of the investment thesis timeline.

If the financing is structured as long-term operating leases, Meta takes on fixed cost obligations that become a headwind if advertising revenue softens — adding operating leverage to an already scale-sensitive business.

rows.name: Amazon Com Inc.
rows.last_memo: Amazon is building a new highly automated warehouse in Texas and opening up its homemade AI chip — Trainium — to more cloud customers. Neither of these is a surprise; both are pieces of a strategy Amazon has been executing for years. The robotic warehouse means fewer people doing
risks: Trainium adoption requires customers to retool AI workflows for non-Nvidia hardware; slower-than-expected developer uptake could limit the competitive moat benefit.
Heavy capex for robotic warehouses and AI chip infrastructure keeps capital intensity elevated; if utilization ramps slowly, ROIC temporarily compresses before the efficiency gains materialize.

rows.name: NVIDIA Corporation
rows.last_memo: A financial media outlet is reporting that Google is working on a new AI chip that could run AI tasks up to 10 times more cheaply than current chips — implicitly taking aim at NVIDIA's expensive GPUs. This is real in the sense that Google and other big tech companies have been bu
risks: If Google's chip achieves commercial-scale deployment with verified 10x inference cost reduction, it could accelerate hyperscaler substitution of NVIDIA GPUs for inference workloads, compressing both volumes and ASPs in the fastest-growing AI compute segment.
AMD is demonstrating real, named design wins with frontier AI labs, increasing the credibility of a multi-vendor AI hardware market that reduces NVIDIA's pricing power over time.

TSMC has issued a warning (specifics not fully available) that may signal capacity constraints, price increases, or geopolitical exposure that could limit NVIDIA's production scale or compress gross margins.

Concurrent competitive and supply-chain headwinds arriving together may amplify customer incentive to accelerate alternative vendor qualification, creating a feedback loop not present when either risk operates alone.

rows.name: International Business Machines Corporation
rows.last_memo: IBM told investors it expects to make less money this year than it previously thought, after also missing expectations in the second quarter of 2026 — essentially a double disappointment. At the same time, IBM bought two companies: Confluent (a data-streaming software firm, close
risks: Mid-year revenue guidance cut signals either Consulting cyclical weakness or Software deceleration — the latter would directly trigger thesis kill criteria and requires urgent segment-level verification.

Net debt/EBITDA of 3.47x already exceeds our 2.5x threshold; the Confluent acquisition and potential HRL purchase price will likely push leverage higher before it improves, reducing financial flexibility.

HRL Laboratories acquisition represents capital deployed into long-duration, speculative quantum/advanced-materials research at a moment when core business execution is under pressure, raising capital allocation discipline concerns.
Two acquisitions in quick succession (Confluent in March, HRL in mid-2026) increase integration complexity and management attention risk during a period of organic underperformance.

rows.name: Shopify Inc.
rows.last_memo: Shopify announced it is connecting its merchant platform to DoorDash, meaning store owners can now offer local same-day delivery without Shopify having to build or own any delivery trucks or warehouses — they just plug into DoorDash's network. This is a good sign: it makes Shopif
risks: DoorDash partnership creates a dependency on a third-party logistics network; if DoorDash changes pricing, availability, or terms, merchants enabled through this channel could face disruption with limited Shopify recourse.

Analyst downgrade, even if valuation-driven, can accelerate institutional selling and increase short-term price volatility, which matters only if Shopify needs to issue equity or if management credibility becomes tied to share price performance.
Dependency on DoorDash's own network reliability and pricing could expose Shopify merchants to service disruptions or cost pass-throughs outside Shopify's control.

If DoorDash deteriorates as a business or changes its partner economics, Shopify merchants relying on this integration could be disrupted, potentially creating merchant dissatisfaction attributed to the Shopify platform.

rows.name: Microsoft Corporation
rows.last_memo: Microsoft deepened its relationship with Mistral, a European AI company, so enterprise customers can now more easily run Mistral's AI models inside Microsoft's Azure cloud. Think of Azure as a shopping mall: Microsoft is adding another anchor tenant. This reinforces the idea that
risks: Multi-model marketplace strategy could commoditize Azure's AI differentiation if customers treat cloud providers as interchangeable inference pipes — though Microsoft's enterprise compliance, identity, and security envelope mitigates this significantly.
No financial terms disclosed means the committee cannot assess whether Microsoft is subsidizing Mistral's Azure compute costs to win the partnership, which would be a margin headwind if material.

If Mistral's models underperform technically relative to OpenAI or Google Gemini, Microsoft's European AI positioning could be weakened despite the investment, having allocated capital to a suboptimal asset.

EU regulatory bodies may scrutinize the deal as a de facto acquisition of influence over a nominally 'independent' European AI champion, creating regulatory friction in a market where Microsoft already faces elevated antitrust attention.

rows.name: Palantir Tech Inc.
rows.last_memo: Palantir's stock dropped about 6% today, but nothing happened at the company — no earnings report, no contract news, no management statement. The stock simply fell, which happens regularly with high-valuation technology names. Think of it like this: if you owned a rental property
risks: Channel partnerships with integrators like SNP can create margin dilution on co-sold deals if revenue-sharing terms are unfavorable; terms were not disclosed.
Reliance on a partner to drive SAP-adjacent pipeline introduces execution dependency on a third party outside Palantir's direct control.

Nvidia partnership could create indirect dependency: if Nvidia's AI hardware market share erodes or enterprise AI spending decelerates, Palantir's growth narrative tied to Nvidia co-selling could be impaired.

SBA fraud initiatives tied to specific political priorities may be de-funded or redirected with administration changes, adding concentration risk within the government segment if this becomes a material revenue line.

rows.name: Alphabet Inc.
rows.last_memo: Alphabet just reported Q2 2026 earnings and the stock fell sharply. The most important thing to understand is not the stock move — it's two structural changes revealed in the quarterly filing. First, Alphabet raised money by selling new shares and a special type of preferred stoc
risks: Alphabet issued mandatory convertible preferred stock at a 6.25% coupon plus common equity dilution to fund Wiz — the preferred will convert to common shares, creating an ongoing dilution overhang.
The Wiz acquisition (~$32B) introduces integration risk, goodwill impairment risk, and potential cultural disruption in a deal larger than any prior Alphabet acquisition.
Structurally elevated capex for AI infrastructure, if Cloud AI monetization lags the investment cycle, could suppress FCF for 2-3+ years, testing the patience of the investment thesis.

Post-earnings stock decline suggests the market may have received negative signals about Search monetization pace or near-term margin trajectory that are not fully visible in the XBRL-only 10-Q content provided.

rows.name: Unitedhealth Group Inc.
rows.last_memo: UnitedHealth's finance chief publicly warned investors about rising healthcare costs after the company reported earnings — and that is a bigger deal than it sounds. The core way UnitedHealth makes money is by collecting insurance premiums and spending less on medical claims than
risks: MCR may be running structurally higher than current pricing assumes, creating a multi-quarter earnings headwind before repricing cycles catch up.
CFO-level public warning may precede formal guidance cuts, creating additional downside to consensus earnings estimates that the forward PE of 19x relies on.
If cost trends reflect broader industry-wide utilization normalization post-COVID, UNH's pricing power advantage is temporarily neutralized, reducing the moat's near-term earnings benefit.
Reputational and political exposure may limit UNH's ability to raise premiums aggressively to offset cost inflation, particularly in Medicare Advantage and ACA-related products.

rows.name: AST SpaceMobile Inc.
rows.last_memo: Two things happened: AT&T said it plans to offer ASTS's satellite service to its customers starting next year, and ASTS borrowed $1 billion to fund operations. The AT&T statement is the most concrete signal yet that a major U.S. phone carrier actually intends to sell this service
risks: The $1B debt adds a fixed-obligation creditor claim senior to equity; if commercial revenue is delayed past 2026, covenant or refinancing pressure could force dilutive equity raises at the worst possible time.
AT&T's 'next year' statement creates a public expectation anchor — if service does not launch in 2026, the credibility hit to both ASTS and the AT&T partnership will be significant and measurable.
Debt financing terms (interest rate, covenants, maturity) are not detailed in available material; unknown covenant structure is a risk factor that warrants due diligence before sizing.
Accelerating satellite staging at Cape implies accelerating launch cadence, which increases near-term capital expenditure requirements and could pressure cash runway sooner than modeled.

rows.name: Tesla Inc.
rows.last_memo: Tesla's sales grew 26% compared to a year ago, which sounds great — but the company actually earned far less from operations, and cash flow turned negative for the quarter. The reason is that Tesla is spending massively more on research and paying its employees (especially Elon M
risks: Stock-based compensation running at ~$4.6B annualized pace in 2026 represents a structural per-share dilution headwind not previously modeled at this magnitude, particularly tied to Musk's new performance grant.

Free cash flow turning negative while the company trades at 346x trailing earnings creates a reflexive risk: if the stock falls significantly, employee retention via equity becomes more expensive in cash-equivalent terms.
PP&E grew $6.7B in one half-year to $47.3B, implying heavy capital expenditure that may not generate returns if autonomous driving timelines slip further.
Tesla disclosed an equity investment in SpaceX and transactions with SpaceX as a related party — this raises governance concerns about capital deployment to affiliated Musk entities.

history.headline: Q2 2026 10-Q filed; earnings triggered stock sell-off; capex surge and equity issuance are the real thesis tests
PLTR drops 6.1% — price move only, no thesis-relevant information
IBM cuts 2026 revenue guidance after Q2 miss; thesis under modest pressure but not broken
AT&T confirms 2026 launch; $1B debt extends runway but adds leverage risk
TSLA Q2 2026: Revenue +26% YoY but operating income collapses 57%; FCF turns negative
Google's TPU efficiency claim is a known risk materializing, not a thesis-breaker — yet
Microsoft-Mistral partnership incrementally supports AI distribution thesis; no structural change
Q2 earnings imminent; thesis intact but reverse-DCF implies ~26% growth priced in
DoorDash integration adds omnichannel depth; analyst downgrade is valuation-driven, not thesis-driven
IBM Q2 earnings imminent; no new data yet — thesis unchanged, monitor for structural signals
BlueBirds 8-10 deployed, 3 more staged: satellite scaling execution progressing as needed
AMD/Anthropic deal and TSMC warning are real but manageable signals, not thesis-killers
Microsoft funds Mistral's EU expansion; modest optionality add, not a thesis mover
Meta's $12B Texas data center bond deal confirms massive AI capex commitment, funded off-balance-sheet
DoorDash integration expands Shopify's logistics layer; incremental, not transformative
Dividend safety headline flags earnings concern; key facts absent — investigate before acting
ASTS raises $1B via 8yr convertible notes to fund satellite buildout; Midland approves 5x factory expansion
Tesla AI/autonomy capex burn headlines arrive as FCF already weak; no new data, but pressure real
BofA flags Azure growth and capex as key watch items — no new data, thesis intact
Anthropic investment optionality is real but not yet a thesis driver; no change warranted
UNH CFO warns on costs post-earnings; MCR pressure confirmed, guidance credibility in question
Meta secures $12B external financing for Texas data centers — capex risk partially offloaded
Break-up speculation signals market anxiety, but thesis hinges on fundamentals not sentiment
ASTS raised $1B in new capital; dilution is real but runway extension matters more at this stage
Capex pressure real but thesis-level damage requires evidence of structural margin compression
PLTR-SNP partnership targets SAP migrations; minor thesis support, no valuation impact
UNH Q2 2026: MCR reset narrative + raised guidance, but structural margin compression is real
IBM shares -3.6% on analyst warnings; thin content warrants investigation, not panic
$1B convertible offering extends runway; ~1.5% dilution less severe than market's 25% reaction implied
Robotaxi headline noise; no new data to move a $1.44T thesis built on autonomous execution
Starbucks DIY AI story is anecdote, not thesis-level evidence of enterprise switching-cost erosion
PLTR: Nvidia partnership + SBA expansion reinforce thesis but don't move intrinsic value at current multiples
UNH beats Q2 2026, raises full-year guidance — execution validates thesis but stock still trades well above fair value
ASTS raises $1B convertible notes (2034) + hints at ~$1B Japan subsidy; dilution concern drives -18% drop
CSU up 6.5% in a day; no fundamental event — noise, not signal
Analyst price-target cuts on weak performance; no structural thesis change evident
DOJ expands antitrust probe into Claritev deal, threatening UNH's integration-via-acquisition playbook
Meta AI infrastructure ambitions intact; two capex headwinds are real but not thesis-altering
DoorDash integration extends Shopify's omnichannel reach; thesis-consistent, not valuation-moving
IBM signals customer spending shift; survey confirms weakening demand — thesis under active stress
BlueBird launch imminent + $150M Midland deal + global regulatory approvals: thesis de-risking on multiple fronts
Jefferies upgrades DECK citing HOKA momentum; supports thesis but adds no new fundamental data
Robotics + Trainium expansion reinforces thesis but is incremental, not transformational
IBM pre-announces Q2 'shortfall' — stock -25%; thesis under acute pressure pending detail
IBM's 'Bob' AI platform updates are thesis-directional but lack monetization data to move the needle
ASTS down 7.8% — pure price noise; no thesis-relevant information in this event
NZ regulatory win is real but immaterial; -8% move is noise on a pre-revenue story
